title: piquant smoked tunisian turkey tagine
description: velvety, herbal tagine featuring smoked turkey neck, served with fluffy couscous and preserved lemon wedges.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the onion, garlic, and ginger and sauté until the onion is translucent.
Add the cumin, coriander, paprika, and chili flakes and stir to combine.
Add the smoked turkey neck and stir to coat with the spice mixture.
Add the preserved lemon, honey, and chicken broth and bring to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook for 1 hour, or until the turkey is tender.
Stir in the chopped fresh herbs and cook for 5 minutes.
Serve the turkey and sauce over couscous and garnish with additional herbs, if desired.
Refrigerate any leftover tagine for up to 1 week and reheat when ready.
To make the mantecare, whip the heavy cream until stiff peaks form and set aside.
To serve, place a portion of the tagine on a plate and top with a dollop of the mantecare.
Garnish with additional herbs and preserved lemon, if desired.
